name: TextureWriter
summary: The TextureWriter class is used primarily for writing the textures used in a SketchUp model out to files as part of an export for use in another application. These methods are usually invoked in this order: #load - load one or more textures from a model into the TextureWriter. #write_all or #write - write the texture(s) to file.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: The #length method is used to determine the number of textures loaded into the texture writer.
methods.remarks: The #length method is used to determine the number of textures loaded into the texture writer. The #count method is an alias for #length.

returns.doc: length - the number of textures loaded in the texture writer

methods.name: filename
methods.summary: The filename method is used to retrieve the original filename for a particular texture.
methods.remarks: The filename method is used to retrieve the original filename for a particular texture.

params.name: handle
params.doc: The index or handle of the texture in the texture writer.

returns.doc: filename - the filename of the texture on the file system. Textures can be generated without filenames, so if the texture doesn't have a name write_all will save the texture to an image file named “i.png”. Where i is the first integer greater than zero which was not already used for a png file name.

methods.name: handle
methods.summary: The handle method is used to retrieve a handle or index for a specific texture in the texture writer.
methods.remarks: The handle method is used to retrieve a handle or index for a specific texture in the texture writer.

params.name: entity
params.doc: A image, component instance, group, or layer.

returns.doc: the index for the entity in the texture writer.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is used to determine the number of textures loaded into the texture writer.
methods.remarks: The #length method is used to determine the number of textures loaded into the texture writer. The #count method is an alias for #length.

returns.doc: length - the number of textures loaded in the texture writer

methods.name: load
methods.summary: Note: If you are passing a face in as the entity argument when loading a texture you will have to specify the second boolean argument, side.
methods.remarks: Note: If you are passing a face in as the entity argument when loading a texture you will have to specify the second boolean argument, side. The argument side specifies which side of the face the texture will be loaded from. The load method is used to load one or more textures into the texture writer for writing out to a file.

params.name: entity
params.doc: Image, component instance, group, or layer to load.

returns.doc: handle - the index or handle of the entity that was loaded

methods.name: write
methods.signature: write(entity, filename) => Integer | write(entity, side, filename) => Integer
methods.summary: Note: If you are passing a face in as the entity argument when writing a texture you will have to specify the boolean argument, side.
methods.remarks: Note: If you are passing a face in as the entity argument when writing a texture you will have to specify the boolean argument, side. The argument side controls the side of the face from which the texture will be sampled before writing it. The write method is used to write an individual textures, within the texture writer, to a file. An entity's texture must have been loaded into the texture writer before this method can be used to write it's texture. This method will return one of the following status messages. (These are constants that are defined by the API.) 0 = FILE_WRITE_OK 1 = FILE_WRITE_FAILED_INVALID_TYPE 2 = FILE_WRITE_FAILED_UNKNOWN

params.name: entity
params.doc: An image, component instance, group, or layer to write.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The name of the file to contain the texture.

returns.doc: status - one of three status messages (see comments.)

methods.name: write_all
methods.summary: The write_all method is used to write all of the textures within the texture writer to files.
methods.remarks: The write_all method is used to write all of the textures within the texture writer to files. It will return one of three status numbers: 0 = FILE_WRITE_OK 1 = FILE_WRITE_FAILED_INVALID_TYPE 2 = FILE_WRITE_FAILED_UNKNOWN

params.name: filename_format
params.doc: true to use 8.3 DOS name format.

returns.doc: status - one of three status messages (see comments.)
